materials: DK yarn in the colour(s) of your choice
A button if you aren’t going to crochet one

rounds.title: Round 1
rounds.rawInstruction: Starting in the 2nd chain from the hook and working in one side of the chain, work as follows, 2 HTC in first stitch, 1 HTC in each of the next 8 stitches, 2HTC in the next stitch, working in the other side of the chain, 2 HTC in the next stitch, 1HTC in each of the next 8 stitches, 2 HTC in the last stitch, join to the 1st HTC. You should have 24 stitches in total. Mark the beginning of your round with a stitch marker.
rounds.summary: Work half treble crochets around both sides of the foundation chain and join.

rounds.title: Round 2-6
rounds.rawInstruction: 1 HTC in each stitch all the way around
rounds.summary: Work one half treble crochet in each stitch around for five rounds.

rounds.title: Round 7
rounds.rawInstruction: 1 DC in each stitch all the way around
rounds.summary: Work one double crochet in each stitch around.

rounds.title: Row 1
rounds.rawInstruction: 1DC, 1DC, CH 2, turn, 1 HTC in the next 10 stitches, CH 2, turn.
rounds.summary: Begin the flap by working double crochets, chaining, turning, and working half treble crochets.

rounds.title: Row 2-9
rounds.rawInstruction: 1 HTC in each stitch all the way across, CH 2, turn
rounds.summary: Work half treble crochets across and chain 2, turn for eight rows.

rounds.title: Row 10
rounds.rawInstruction: 2HTCtog, 1HTC, 1HTC, CH2, skip the next 2 stitches, 1HTC, 1HTC, 2HTCtog, CH2, turn
rounds.summary: Create the buttonhole by decreasing, chaining, skipping stitches, and decreasing again.

rounds.title: Row 11
rounds.summary: Work half treble crochets across and fasten off.

rounds.title: Start with a magic ring
rounds.summary: Create a magic ring to begin the button.

rounds.title: Round 1
rounds.summary: Work twelve treble crochets into the ring and join.

rounds.title: Round 2
rounds.summary: Slip stitch into each stitch around.

rounds.title: Line the button up with where the button hole on the flap lies when in position at the front of the pouch, (you can mark this with a stitch marker) and sew into place.
rounds.rawInstruction: Line the button up with where the button hole on the flap lies when in position at the front of the pouch, (you can mark this with a stitch marker) and sew into place.
rounds.summary: Align and sew the button to the flap.

rounds.title: Weave in the ends.
rounds.summary: Secure and hide all yarn tails.
